title: Fix dashboard data display + replace start button icon
description: Overview and System tabs show dashes instead of real data. The pulse-data endpoint crashes because req-016 has completedAt as an ISO string, causing a TypeError in count_completed(). This crashes the entire build, so the fallback stub (all dashes) gets served. Also replace the start arrow button with a play icon matching the + fab style.

subtasks.title: Fix completedAt type mismatch crashing pulse-data
subtasks.description: req-016 has completedAt as ISO string instead of int, crashing count_completed(). Normalize in load_mission_control_jobs().

title: Overview Pen on jobs

Overview Pen on jobs

Pen should only be there when you take the job I've written and you're rewriting it. So it's signal that the job will be back with tasks in a few seconds. then when back, no more pen please.

title: Update on logic and colours
description:
Update on logic and colours

Jobs are green, tasks are blue. So the REQ number should always be green. can change shade based on to do and done... but green. The sub tasks have to be blue. Therefore the overview activity will match the colour code for our system. The word : to do / in overview should be replaced with : Current jobs
It should show only when at least one job is there, otherwise, clean top 8 squares only in overview.
details:
Update on logic and colours

Jobs are green, tasks are blue. So the REQ number should always be green. can change shade based on to do and done... but green. The sub tasks have to be blue. Therefore the overview activity will match the colour code for our system. The word : to do / in overview should be replaced with : Current jobs
It should show only when at least one job is there, otherwise, clean top 8 squares only in overview.

title: Enrich dispatched agent display with type, model, session name, and REQ link
description:
Currently the dispatched agents section in System tab shows raw command names (pi-acp, codex-acp) and session ID fragments. The ACP session JSON has much richer data that should be surfaced:

1. Map agent commands to friendly names: pi-acp → Pi, codex-acp → Codex, claude-agent-acp → Claude Code
2. Show the model each agent is running (from acpx.current_model_id)
3. Show session name/label if available
4. Link to REQ if the session is working on one
5. Backend: expose agent_command, name, model, cwd from ACP session data in pulse-data API
6. Frontend: render the enriched data in dispatched agent lines

title: Enrich ACP session display — link sessions to agent definitions, show type/model/task context
description:
The dispatched agents section now shows friendly names and models, but sessions should be linked to agent definition files (.md in the vault) and show more context about what the agent is working on.

1. Create agent definition files in the vault (Pi.md, Codex.md, Claude Code.md) with role, capabilities, default model
2. Link ACP sessions to their agent MD files — MC could show a reference or icon
3. Show what task/REQ the session is working on (parse from session name or link to active job)
4. Consider session lifecycle: active sessions show in dispatched, closed sessions could show in a history log
5. Session metadata in MC could include: agent type icon, model badge, task reference, duration, status

title: REQ-046: Research Offline Voice Systems
description:
Research and evaluate offline STT/TTS systems for Mac Studio M4 Max 64GB. Focus on highest quality and responsiveness for local inference.

title: Add Open in Obsidian links to MC vault viewer
description: Add obsidian://open URI deep links to Research tab and Logs/Vault tab. Click a file → Open in Obsidian button to jump to the note in the Obsidian app for editing, backlinks, graph view. Use obsidian://open?vault=Mission%20Control&file=<encoded-path>.

title: Add Open in Obsidian links to REQ cards
description: Add obsidian://open URI deep links to REQ job cards on Overview. Each card gets an Open in Obsidian button that jumps to the vault note (REQ/REQ-XXX.md) for full history, backlinks, and editing.

title: Redesign Research page — list-only + modal viewer + search-as-launch
description:
1. Research tab shows only a clean list of research notes (no split-pane content viewer)
2. Clicking a note opens a modal popup with the content, formatted like Obsidian (frontmatter block, wikilinks styled, heading hierarchy)
3. Search bar at top is for launching new research — type a topic, hit Enter, creates a research job (Alfred researches online, files note in vault Research/ folder)
4. Remove the current two-column layout (tree + content panel)

title: Simplify System page — single source of truth, kill alfred-status
description: Remove /api/alfred-status endpoint and 5s polling. Derive Alfred card status from job data only: working-phase jobs exist = Working, otherwise = Available. No more drift between status file and actual state.

title: Research: Hermes Agent — purpose and advantages
description: Research the Hermes agent project. What is it, what problem does it solve, and what are its advantages over existing approaches? Create a research note in the MC vault with YAML frontmatter, wikilinks, and category tags. Update the Research Index.

title: Agent card: replace queue depth with sync countdown
description: In the agent card (bottom right), replace the queue depth number with a server sync countdown timer. Remove the queue metric entirely. Adapt the style from square to rectangle to fit the countdown display. The sync loop should be visually represented — showing seconds until next pulse refresh.

Agent card

In the agent card at the bottom right, the queue cut out number, can we do the countdown for the server sync to show in there instead? Doesn’t have to stay square go rectangle and adapt style. Remove the queue metric and now we could see the server sync loop visually.
